description: Misconception registry for Linguics. A misconception is an ERROR PATTERN, the structured abstraction above a set of surface wrong-forms (capisciamo, finisciamo, preferisciamo all embody one misconception: stem_change.isc_over_extension). Misconceptions are ORTHOGONAL to the skill buckets: one misconception spans many buckets (over-extending a stem change appears in ire_isc, go_verbs, dittongo), and one bucket can be failed by several misconceptions. They are tagged at the point of detection: a grammar item's must_not_include entry (in object form) carries an optional `misconception` field naming an id from this registry; when that wrong form fires, the housing records the usual bucket miss AND a misconception event. Misconception events aggregate on their own axis, surfaced in a SEPARATE drill-down stats view (never inline during questions, so tagging carries no leak risk). The marquee long-term value is the CROSS-KIND families (agreement, accent, regularisation): the same misconception observed across adjectives, participles, articles, pronouns, and verbs. Governance mirrors the glossary and themes registries: architecture-owned and canonical; authors tag against it and propose additions via misconception_suggestions_<topic>.json; architecture ratifies. This v1 seed is populated from the present-indicative formation work plus the general families; it is designed to grow as every topic comes online. Many specifics here can be bootstrapped from the `common_miss` prose attributes already authored across the bucket trees.

agreement.label: Agreement
agreement.description: Wrong gender, number, or person where two elements must agree. The flagship cross-kind family: will be populated by adjectives (rossa/rosso), past participles (andata/andato), articles (il/la), pronouns (lo/la), demonstratives, and subject-verb agreement.

regularisation.label: Regularisation
regularisation.description: Treating an irregular form as if it followed the regular pattern. Cross-kind: irregular verbs, irregular plurals, irregular comparatives.

orthography.label: Orthography (sound-preserving spelling)
orthography.description: Spelling adjustments whose job is to keep the written form consistent with the sound. Cross-kind: verb endings (cerco/cerchi), plural spelling, and similar.

accent_silent_letter.label: Accents and silent letters
accent_silent_letter.description: Written accents and silent letters that are obligatory and often meaning-bearing. Cross-kind: appears anywhere accented or h-bearing forms occur.

stress.label: Word stress
stress.description: Misplaced spoken or written stress. Cross-kind, and links to the proposed word-stress vocabulary dimension (see OPEN_QUESTIONS).

stem_change.label: Stem change (over / under application)
stem_change.description: Over-applying or under-applying a conditioned stem change. Mostly verb morphology, but the over/under-application shape recurs elsewhere.

conjugation_class.label: Conjugation-class confusion
conjugation_class.description: Applying one conjugation class's ending or pattern to a verb of another class.

word_order.label: Word order
word_order.description: Reserved cross-kind family (adjective position, clitic position, etc.). No specifics seeded yet; populated as those topics come online.

register.label: Register
register.description: Reserved cross-kind family (formal/informal mismatch). No specifics seeded yet.

false_friend.label: False friend
false_friend.description: Reserved cross-kind (vocab) family. No specifics seeded yet; populated by the vocab side later.

auxiliary_choice.label: Auxiliary choice
auxiliary_choice.description: Reserved cross-tense family (essere vs avere). No specifics seeded yet; will be populated by the passato prossimo / compound-tense work.

misconceptions.label: Reflexive pronoun wrong for the subject
misconceptions.description: Using a reflexive pronoun that doesn't agree with the subject's person (mi lavi for ti lavi). A subject-agreement error; the first foothold of the agreement family on the verb side.

misconceptions.label: Irregular verb regularised
misconceptions.description: Conjugating an irregular verb as if it were regular, ignoring the suppletive or contracted paradigm. Recurs in every tense.

misconceptions.label: -isc- added in noi/voi
misconceptions.description: Over-extending the -isc- infix into the 1pl/2pl, where it never appears, by analogy with the singular and 3pl.

misconceptions.label: -isc- omitted where required
misconceptions.description: Failing to insert -isc- in the singular or 3pl of an -isc- verb.

misconceptions.label: -g- inserted in the wrong person
misconceptions.description: Carrying the inserted -g- of a -go verb into a person that should not have it (the 1pl/2pl, or a regular-stem form).

misconceptions.label: -g- omitted where required
misconceptions.description: Failing to insert the -g- in the 1sg/3pl of a -go verb.

misconceptions.label: Diphthong kept in noi/voi
misconceptions.description: Keeping the stressed-stem diphthong in the 1pl/2pl, where stress moves to the ending and the plain stem returns.

misconceptions.label: Diphthong lost in the singular
misconceptions.description: Failing to diphthongise the stressed stem vowel in the singular or 3pl.

misconceptions.label: -ano 3pl on an -ere/-ire verb
misconceptions.description: Using the -are 3pl ending -ano on an -ere or -ire verb, which takes -ono.

misconceptions.label: -ono 3pl on an -are verb
misconceptions.description: Using the -ere/-ire 3pl ending -ono on an -are verb, which takes -ano.

misconceptions.label: h omitted on -care/-gare
misconceptions.description: Dropping the h that -care/-gare verbs insert before -i endings to keep the c/g hard, which changes the consonant sound.

misconceptions.label: Redundant i kept on -ciare/-giare
misconceptions.description: Keeping the stem i of a -ciare/-giare verb before an ending that already begins with i, producing a wrong double i.

misconceptions.label: Wrong i-retention on -iare by stress
misconceptions.description: Mishandling the stress-governed i of -iare verbs: keeping it when unstressed (studii) or dropping it when stressed (invi).

misconceptions.label: Silent h dropped on avere
misconceptions.description: Dropping the obligatory silent h on the present forms of avere, producing a different real word (o, ai, a, anno).

misconceptions.label: Written accent omitted
misconceptions.description: Omitting an obligatory written accent, often producing a different word (e for è) or an unmarked vowel (puo for può, da for dà). Strongly cross-cutting.

misconceptions.label: 3pl stressed on the penult
misconceptions.description: Misplacing the 3pl stress onto the penult (parlAno) instead of the antepenult (pArlano). Prosodic; links to the proposed word-stress vocab dimension.
